basicDetails.schemeName: Promotion of Exportable surplus of identified spices in NE: Cultivation of NE ginger (Ha)

schemeContent.briefDescription: The objective of the scheme is to promote the cultivation of ginger in the North Eastern region by supporting farmers with financial assistance for quality planting material, enhancing productivity and quality, and increasing the exportable surplus of spices from the region.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The ‘Promotion of Exportable Surplus of Identified Spices in the North East: Cultivation of Ginger’, implemented by the Spices Board, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India, financial assistance is provided at 50% of the cost of planting material, subject to a maximum of ₹30,000 per hectare. The scheme is applicable to ginger growers in the North Eastern region with land holdings ranging from 0.10 ha to 8 ha, including Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, and other North Eastern states. Designated officials will conduct final inspections and recommend eligible cases.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - 50% of cost of planting material subject to maximum of ₹ 30,000/- per Ha - Ginger growers of NE having land holding size of 0.10 Ha to 8 Ha

The scheme is applicable to ginger growers in the North Eastern region with land holdings ranging from 0.10 ha to 8 ha, including Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, and other North Eastern states. Designated officials will conduct final inspections and recommend eligible cases.
50% of cost of planting material subject to maximum of ₹ 30,000/- per Ha
Ginger growers of NE having land holding size of 0.10 Ha to 8 Ha
Registration
Step 1: The applicant should visit the
ServicesPlus
website.
Step 2: Click on the “
Register”
option on the homepage.
Step 3: Fill out the registration form by entering Full Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, selecting State, and Captcha.
Step 4: Review all details carefully and submit the registration form.
Step 5: An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number or email; enter the OTP and click “Verify.”
Step 6: Registration will be completed successfully, and a confirmation message will be displayed or sent via email.
Login
Step 1: The applicant should visit the ServicesPlus website and click on
“Login.”
Step 2: Enter your Email ID, Password, and Captcha.
Step 3: Select the relevant service/scheme name.
Step 4: Fill in the required details in the application form.
Step 5: Submit the application.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Registration** Step 1: The applicant should visit the [ServicesPlus](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/) website. Step 2: Click on the “[Register” ](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/citizenRegistration.html)option on the homepage. Step 3: Fill out the registration form by entering Full Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, selecting State, and Captcha. Step 4: Review all details carefully and submit the registration form. Step 5: An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number or email; enter the OTP and click “Verify.” Step 6: Registration will be completed successfully, and a confirmation message will be displayed or sent via email. **Login** Step 1: The applicant should visit the ServicesPlus website and click on [“Login.”](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/loginWindow.do) Step 2: Enter your Email ID, Password, and Captcha. Step 3: Select the relevant service/scheme name. Step 4: Fill in the required details in the application form. Step 5: Submit the application.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a ginger grower from Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, or other North Eastern states. 1. Eligibility is subject to final inspection and recommendation by designated officials.

children.text: The applicant must be a ginger grower from Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, or other North Eastern states.
Eligibility is subject to final inspection and recommendation by designated officials.
